title: High-country approach and board calm phase
summary: Fifty-sixth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-1 wave strengthens the sense of approaching the inland high country, then keeps the active board calm and confident through the existing season shell.

execution_order.why_now: The inland chapter wants a stronger sense of approach after the season-two shell settles.
Existing travel and station seams can do more to stage the inland region without shell growth.
The inland approach should feel warmer and more intentional, not louder.
Once the inland approach reads better, the next gain is a calmer active-board rhythm.
A quieter board can keep the player oriented without planner drift.
The board must stay helpful without getting denser.

guardrails: Do not add a new planner page.
Do not add another persistent HUD strip.
Reuse the current season, routes, expedition, and world-map seams.
Prefer wording and tiny state shifts over larger shell growth.
acceptance_focus: The inland chapter feels more deliberately approached.
The active board feels calmer and more confident.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-1.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-high-country-approach-and-board-calm-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 1's next long-push wave after the current season-two shell work: strengthen the high-country approach through existing seams, then add one compact board-calm follow-on without opening a new planner surface.
